title: LEGO® Group Play Ideation Internship 2026 in London (Paid): Apply for a Creative Innovation Internship in the Creative Play Lab
summary: The LEGO® Group Play Ideation Internship 2026 is now open for applications from students and recent graduates who are passionate about creativity, innovation, concept development, and the future of play. Based at the LEGO® Group s London Hub, this paid internship offers an exciting opportunity to work with the Creative Play Lab , where interns will collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to invent new play experiences for children around the world. Starting in September 2026 , the internship provides hands-on experience in early-stage ideation, creative problem-solving, concept development, and prototyping. Interns will work alongside designers, researchers, engineers, innovators, and technologists while developing their own creative skills and contributing to the next generation of LEGO® play experiences. If you are an aspiring creative thinker who enjoys exploring what if? ideas and turning imagination into reality, this internship could be the perfect opportunity to launch your career. About the LEGO® Group Creative Play Lab The Creative Play Lab is LEGO® Group s forward-looking innovation hub dedicated to imagining the future of play. The team focuses on developing groundbreaking play experiences by combining: Creative experimentation Advanced technologies Research into children s play behaviours Design thinking Rapid prototyping Cross-disciplinary collaboration Rather than simply improving existing products, the Creative Play Lab explores entirely new ways children can play, learn, and engage with creative experiences. Its mission is to ensure LEGO® continues to inspire imagination for generations to come. About the Play Ideation Internship As a Play Ideation Intern, you will become part of the Play Discovery department, where innovation begins. Interns contribute fresh perspectives, cultural insights, creative energy, and original thinking while learning directly from experienced professionals across multiple disciplines. Throughout the internship, participants will work on: Personal curiosity projects Team innovation missions Creative capability-building initiatives Concept exploration Early-stage prototype development Each day presents new challenges designed to stretch creativity and encourage experimentation. Meet the Play Ideation Team The Play Ideation Team specializes in transforming early concepts into exciting possibilities. Their work includes: Exploring future play opportunities Developing early-stage concepts Helping teams visualize ideas Testing new creative directions Building playful experiences Creating environments that encourage innovation and collaboration The team s philosophy revolves around asking What if? and transforming those questions into the next generation of LEGO® experiences. Internship Details Position: Play Ideation Intern Location: London, United Kingdom Department: Creative Play Lab – Play Discovery Management Level: Support Category: Administration Facilities Job ID: 0000036028 Internship Duration Start Date: 7 September 2026 Duration: Four months (until the end of December 2026) Extension: Possible, depending on business needs Working Arrangement: Full-time or Part-time Responsibilities Interns will actively contribute to creative projects and innovation initiatives. Typical responsibilities include: Supporting creative ideation workshops. Facilitating brainstorming sessions. Transforming early ideas into visual concepts. Exploring independent creative directions. Developing innovative play concepts. Creating concept visualizations. Building prototypes. Encouraging teams to think beyond conventional ideas. Collaborating with multidisciplinary teams. Every assignment is designed to strengthen creativity, collaboration, and innovation skills. What LEGO® Is Looking For Successful applicants will demonstrate three essential qualities. Curiosity Applicants should: Ask thoughtful questions. Explore new possibilities. Investigate challenges deeply. Maintain an experimental mindset. Constantly wonder What if? Bravery Interns should feel comfortable: Sharing original ideas. Receiving constructive feedback. Working in uncertain environments. Collaborating with diverse teams. Taking creative risks. Focus Candidates should be able to: Adapt to changing priorities. Balance multiple projects. Work independently. Deliver creative solutions under evolving requirements. Desired Skills Although not mandatory, experience in any of the following areas will strengthen an application: Sketching Graphic design Animation Concept art LEGO® building Videography Video editing Music Fashion Dance Illustration Facilitation Cultural research Creative hobbies LEGO® values diverse creative experiences and encourages applicants to showcase their unique talents. Application Requirements Applicants must submit four separate PDF documents . 1. A4 Landscape Poster Design an original A4 landscape poster explaining why you are an excellent fit for the internship. Applicants may use: Photography Typography Illustration Colour Graphic design Visual storytelling 2. Curriculum Vitae (CV) Provide an up-to-date CV highlighting education, skills, and relevant experience. 3. Portfolio Submit a comprehensive portfolio demonstrating: Personal projects Academic work Creative experiments Hobbies Design thinking Passion projects 4. Cover Letter Applicants must submit a bespoke cover letter of no more than 200 words . LEGO® encourages originality and authenticity rather than generic applications. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: Saturday, 18 July 2026 Late applications will not be considered. Because applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Internship Benefits Successful interns will enjoy numerous professional and personal benefits. These include: Career Development Work alongside world-class innovators. Learn from multidisciplinary professionals. Build an international professional network. Gain real-world innovation experience. Creative Environment Interns will work inside the LEGO® Group s London Hub, including the highly innovative Red Brick area dedicated to creative experimentation. Collaboration with Children Participants will have opportunities to co-create with children, whose creativity and imagination inspire LEGO® innovation. Employee Benefits Eligible interns may also receive: Enhanced family care leave. Life insurance. Disability insurance. Mental health and wellbeing resources. Employee discounts on LEGO® products. Performance bonus (where eligible). Hybrid working arrangements, averaging three office days per week. Diversity and Inclusion The LEGO® Group is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected. Applications are welcomed from individuals of all backgrounds regardless of: Gender Gender identity Race Ethnicity National origin Religion Disability Sexual orientation Age The company also maintains a strong commitment to children s rights and child safeguarding. Successful candidates working closely with children may be required to complete child safeguarding background screening before employment. Why You Should Apply The Play Ideation Internship is much more than a traditional internship. It is an opportunity to work at one of the world s most innovative toy companies while helping shape the future of creative play. Whether your interests lie in design, storytelling, innovation, art, technology, or creative problem-solving, this internship offers an environment where imagination is encouraged and original ideas are celebrated. If you dream of transforming creative ideas into experiences that inspire millions of children worldwide, this internship offers an ideal starting point. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their application in English through the official LEGO® Careers portal. Ensure you upload: An A4 landscape poster. Your CV. A portfolio. A tailored cover letter (maximum 200 words). Apply Here Applications must be completed before Saturday, 18 July 2026 . For more information, visit the official website Discover more global paid internship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post LEGO® Group Play Ideation Internship 2026 in London (Paid): Apply for a Creative Innovation Internship in the Creative Play Lab appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026: Fully Funded Residency for Emerging U.S. Artists Exploring Mental Health and Well-Being
summary: The Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026 is now accepting applications from emerging artists across the United States who are passionate about using creativity to advance conversations around youth mental health, well-being, healing, and social change. As Hopelab launches its inaugural Artist-in-Residence Program, it is inviting four emerging artists to spend three months developing new or existing creative projects that explore the intersection of art, liberatory mental health, youth well-being, and community transformation . The residency combines financial support, mentorship, creative immersion, professional development, and public engagement, providing artists with the resources needed to deepen their artistic practice while contributing to broader cultural conversations about youth well-being. If you are an emerging artist committed to creating meaningful work that inspires healing and systemic change, this residency offers an exceptional opportunity to develop your vision with support from leading artists, researchers, and innovators. About the Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program Hopelab believes that meaningful social change begins by changing the stories people tell about themselves and their communities. Historically, artists have played a central role in every movement for justice by imagining new possibilities before society was ready to embrace them. Through storytelling, music, visual arts, literature, and other creative forms, artists help communities process grief, celebrate joy, and imagine healthier futures. Recognizing this power, Hopelab created its Artist-in-Residence Program to engage young artists in shaping new narratives around youth mental health and well-being. Rather than focusing solely on research or policy, the residency centers creativity as an essential tool for healing, liberation, and cultural transformation. Participants will have the opportunity to create original work—or complete existing projects—that reflects the intersection of: Art Mental health Youth well-being Community healing Social justice Narrative change Cultural transformation Collective care Future-building Why Hopelab Created This Residency Hopelab recognizes that dominant narratives surrounding youth mental health have often excluded the voices and lived experiences of young people themselves. This residency aims to change that by empowering artists to tell stories that: Challenge existing assumptions. Expand public understanding of well-being. Inspire hope and imagination. Promote healing-centered approaches. Encourage systemic transformation. Center communities historically overlooked by traditional mental health systems. By bringing artists into conversation with researchers, youth advocates, and technologists, Hopelab hopes to create new cultural narratives that support healthier futures for young people. What Residents Will Receive Selected artists will receive a comprehensive package of financial and professional support throughout the residency. 1. Living Stipend Residents will receive a $7,500 living stipend to support their basic living expenses during the three-month residency. 2. Materials Budget Each artist will receive an additional $2,500 materials budget . The budget is intentionally flexible and artist-directed, allowing residents to purchase the materials, equipment, or creative resources most relevant to their artistic practice. 3. Professional Mentorship Artists will receive mentorship from: A practicing professional artist. The Hopelab team. The Djerassi Resident Artists Program team. This guidance is designed to strengthen both artistic development and professional growth. 4. Fully Funded Creative Retreat Residents will participate in a one-week immersive retreat at the renowned Djerassi Resident Artists Program in Northern California s Santa Cruz Mountains. Hopelab will fully cover: Travel expenses Accommodation Meals The retreat provides uninterrupted time for artistic exploration while building meaningful relationships with fellow artists. 5. Access to Experts Participants will connect with Hopelab s network of: Researchers Mental health experts Youth advocates Technologists Creative leaders These interdisciplinary conversations are intended to enrich participants creative work and expand their perspectives. 6. Public Showcase Artists will present their completed work during a public showcase in San Francisco in November 2026 . Hopelab will cover: Travel Accommodation Meals for participation in the showcase. 7. Professional Support Participants will also receive: A professional letter of support Documentation of their completed artistic work These materials can strengthen future grant, residency, fellowship, and exhibition applications. 8. Artist Database Every applicant—whether selected or not—will be included in Hopelab s digital artist database, increasing visibility within Hopelab s creative network. Who Hopelab Is Looking For Hopelab seeks a diverse cohort of emerging artists who: Are between 18 and 29 years old. Live anywhere in the United States. Work across one or multiple artistic disciplines. Explore themes related to mental health and well-being. Come from communities historically underserved by mental health systems. Maintain strong connections to their communities through organizing, culture, mutual aid, or collective care. Demonstrate originality and a compelling artistic vision. Are committed to participating in every aspect of the residency. Importantly, applicants do not need a university degree or previous institutional recognition. Selection emphasizes: Lived experience Community engagement Artistic vision Commitment to the residency Potential for impact Residency Timeline The residency follows the schedule below: June 22, 2026 – Applications open July 17, 2026 – Application deadline July 27–31, 2026 – Finalist interviews and selection announcement August 10 – November 6, 2026 – Three-month residency with biweekly cohort meetings and guest lectures October 5–11, 2026 – In-person residency at Djerassi Resident Artists Program Around November 5, 2026 – Public showcase in San Francisco Application Requirements Applicants must submit: A work sample An artist statement Community context information These materials help the selection committee understand each applicant s creative vision, lived experience, and relationship with their community. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Artistic vision Creative potential Lived experience Community rootedness Alignment with the residency mission Commitment to full participation Rather than focusing on formal credentials, Hopelab values authenticity, imagination, and a demonstrated commitment to creating meaningful work. Why You Should Apply The Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program offers much more than financial support. Participants will gain: Funding to pursue ambitious creative projects. Professional mentorship. National visibility. Access to interdisciplinary experts. A supportive artist community. Fully funded travel and residency experiences. Opportunities to showcase work publicly. Long-term professional connections. For emerging artists interested in shaping conversations around youth mental health and social transformation, this residency represents a rare opportunity to combine artistic practice with meaningful cultural impact. How to Apply Interested artists should submit their application through Hopelab s official Artist-in-Residence application portal before the deadline. Applicants should ensure they prepare: Work sample. Artist statement. Community context information. Applications must be submitted by July 17, 2026 . Apply Here Final Thoughts The Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026 is a unique opportunity for emerging U.S.-based artists to receive generous financial support, mentorship, and national exposure while creating work that advances youth mental health and well-being. By bringing together creativity, community, and research, Hopelab aims to empower a new generation of artists whose work can inspire healing, challenge dominant narratives, and imagine healthier futures for young people. Artists who are passionate about storytelling, social change, and community well-being are strongly encouraged to apply before July 17, 2026 . For more information, visit the official website. Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Hopelab Artist-in-Residence Program 2026: Fully Funded Residency for Emerging U.S. Artists Exploring Mental Health and Well-Being appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations World Health Organization Internship Program 2026 | Paid Internships for Students & Recent Graduates Worldwide
summary: Looking to launch your career in global health? The World Health Organization (WHO) Internship Programme 2026 offers students and recent graduates a unique opportunity to gain practical experience while contributing to one of the world s leading public health organizations. The WHO Internship Programme provides hands-on exposure to international health initiatives, allowing interns to work alongside global experts, strengthen their professional skills, and contribute to improving health outcomes worldwide. With opportunities available throughout the year across multiple countries, this paid internship is ideal for aspiring professionals in public health, medicine, communications, engineering, law, data science, administration, and many other fields. About the WHO Internship Programme The WHO Internship Programme is designed to develop the next generation of global health professionals by providing meaningful learning experiences in technical and administrative areas. Interns work in a multicultural environment alongside experts from diverse backgrounds while supporting WHO s mission to promote health, keep the world safe, and serve vulnerable populations. The programme helps participants gain valuable insight into international health systems, policy development, emergency response, research, and organizational operations while building professional networks across the globe. Why Apply for the WHO Internship? The WHO Internship Programme offers participants the opportunity to: Gain practical experience in a globally recognized organization. Work alongside leading public health and international development professionals. Contribute to projects addressing global health challenges. Build professional networks with experts from around the world. Develop technical, analytical, and leadership skills. Experience a multicultural and inclusive working environment. WHO Internship Benefits Successful applicants may receive several benefits, including: Paid internship with financial support. Living allowance for eligible interns. Medical and accident insurance coverage during the internship. Lunch vouchers at selected duty stations. Professional supervision and mentorship. Practical experience working on international health programmes. Exposure to cutting-edge public health initiatives and health systems. Internship Duration The WHO Internship Programme lasts: Minimum: 6 weeks Maximum: 24 weeks The exact duration depends on the department and internship assignment. Who Can Apply? The WHO Internship Programme is open to: Undergraduate students Master s students PhD students Recent graduates Eligibility Criteria To be eligible, applicants must: Be at least 20 years old at the time of application. Be currently enrolled in an undergraduate, graduate, or postgraduate programme, or have recently graduated. Be studying at an institution recognized in the World Higher Education Database (WHED) . Provide proof of their academic qualifications. Be fluent in at least one of WHO s official working languages. Hold a valid passport from a WHO Member State. Have no immediate family relationship with a WHO staff member. Not have previously participated in the WHO Internship Programme. Required Documents Applicants should prepare the following documents: Updated CV or résumé Academic transcripts Academic letter of reference Passport details Application acknowledgment WHO Medical Certificate (required before the internship begins) Any additional documents requested for the specific internship How to Apply To apply for the WHO Internship Programme: Visit the official WHO Careers portal . Search for available internship opportunities. Select the internship that matches your qualifications. Complete the online application form. Provide your personal and academic information. Upload the required documents. Complete the Declaration of Interest form (where applicable). Review your application carefully and submit it. You will receive an email confirming your application submission. Application Deadline WHO internship opportunities are advertised throughout the year , with each vacancy having its own application deadline. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. WHO Internship Opportunities Available This Week 1. Internship – Health Emergencies Location: Geneva, Switzerland Organization: HQ/EPM – Epidemic Pandemic Management Contract Type: Intern Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 APPLY NOW 2. Internship – Communications and Advocacy Location: Berlin, Germany Organization: HQ/EIS – Health Emergency Intelligence Surveillance Contract Type: Intern Application Deadline: 30 July 2026 APPLY NOW 3. AI Engineering Intern – Data Engineering, Full-Stack Development, Quality Evaluation Location: Istanbul, Türkiye Organization: EU/BOS – Division of Business Operations Contract Type: Intern Application Deadline: 21 July 2026 APPLY NOW 4. Internship – Health Systems, Noncommunicable Diseases Communications Location: Port Louis, Mauritius Organization: WHO Country Office Mauritius Contract Type: Intern Application Deadline: 21 July 2026 APPLY NOW 5. Legal Intern Location: Cairo, Egypt Organization: EM/BOS – Business Operations Contract Type: Intern Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 APPLY NOW Why You Shouldn t Miss This Opportunity The WHO Internship Programme is one of the world s most prestigious international internship opportunities for students and recent graduates. Whether your interests lie in public health, medicine, engineering, communications, artificial intelligence, law, policy, or administration, the programme offers invaluable international experience, professional development, and the opportunity to contribute to improving health outcomes worldwide. With paid benefits, mentorship from global experts, and opportunities available in multiple countries, the WHO Internship Programme can be an excellent stepping stone toward a career in international organizations and global health. Apply Now Applications are submitted through the official WHO Careers Portal . Since new internship opportunities are posted regularly throughout the year, applicants are encouraged to check the portal frequently and apply before the listed deadlines. APPLY NOW OFFICIAL LINK The post United Nations World Health Organization Internship Program 2026 | Paid Internships for Students Recent Graduates Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OECD Research Fellowships Research Fellowships 2027 | Fully Funded International Research Fellowships for Agricultural, Forestry & Fisheries Scientists
summary: CRP Applications for Fellowships Applications are now open for the OECD Co-operative Research Programme (CRP) Research Fellowships 2027 , offering scientists the opportunity to undertake 6–26 weeks of funded international research in another participating country. The programme supports cutting-edge research that advances sustainable agriculture, forestry, fisheries, food systems, climate resilience and natural resource management while fostering global scientific collaboration. Key Details Host Organisation: OECD Co-operative Research Programme (CRP) Programme: CRP Research Fellowships 2027 Funding: Travel grant including return economy airfare, €600–€650 weekly subsistence allowance , and a €165 terminal allowance Duration: 6–26 weeks Eligible Applicants: Researchers working in agriculture, forestry or fisheries with at least four years of postdoctoral experience (or equivalent scientific expertise in exceptional cases). The programme is not intended for PhD students. Research Location: Another participating OECD CRP member country Eligible Countries: Researchers based in participating CRP countries, including the United Kingdom, United States, Canada, Australia, Brazil, Japan, Germany, France, Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland, Spain, Ireland, New Zealand and others. Research Areas: Sustainable agriculture, food security, climate change, biodiversity, forestry, fisheries, aquaculture, soil health, livestock systems, ecosystem services, Indigenous knowledge, water quality and agricultural innovation. Application Deadline: 10 September 2026 (Midnight, Paris Time) Results Announced: January 2027 Fellowship Start: From March 2027 Application Fee: None Application process for fellowships The Co-operative Research Programme’s call for applications for funding research fellowship grants in 2027 is now OPEN. The deadline to apply is Thursday 10 September 2026, midnight, Paris time. General information Applicants must be residents in one of the countries participating in our Programme. Please consult the files for general information on the country s research institutes and universities. Please note that: Türkiye is a member until 24 December 2026 and will not participate in the call for applications for events and fellowships to be funded in 2027. Brazil is a member of the CRP as of 1 July 2026 , so Brazil is eligible to the call for applications for fellowships to be funded in 2027. Ukraine is on ACCESSION STAGE . Should you want to have speakers from this country, please contact TAD.PROG@oecd.org to be updated on its status. Please consult the Event Guidelines and Conditions, Selection Criteria and the CRP Action Areas in the documents below. Applications relevant to the work of the OECD Committee for Agriculture and other bodies are particularly welcome: • Sustainable and resilient productivity growth and food security and nutrition • Climate risks, reducing emissions from agriculture and food systems, carbon sequestration in agriculture, forestry and land use • Halting and reversing forest loss and land degradation • Reducing the negative environmental impacts of livestock production and practices harmful to animal health and welfare, investigating the positive contribution livestock can make to soil quality and management, biodiversity and livelihoods • Biodiversity, enhancing ecosystem services • Improving soil health and water and air quality, including through agro-ecological and other innovative, context specific approaches • Innovations in the transfer and development of agricultural knowledge, including Indigenous and traditional knowledge • Fisheries and aquaculture productivity, sustainability and resilience Previous research fellowships are available for review. Testimonials from past fellows and their hosts can also be reviewed on the CRP home page (scroll down to the dedicated section). Eligibility Requirements Beyond being a researcher in agriculture, forestry or fisheries, applicants should also note that they must: Be employed at an institution located in a participating CRP country. Undertake the fellowship in another participating CRP country. Normally have at least four years of postdoctoral experience . The programme is not intended for PhD students , although exceptional applicants without a PhD but with equivalent expertise and a strong publication record may be considered. Have employer approval before submitting the application. Have continued employment (or ongoing scientific affiliation) after the fellowship ends. Previous CRP fellows may only apply again five years after receiving their first fellowship. What Does the Fellowship Cover? The OECD CRP Fellowship provides a travel lump-sum allowance consisting of three components: Return economy-class travel between the fellow s home institution and host institution Weekly subsistence allowance of €600–€650 per week (depending on the cost of living in the host country) A €165 terminal allowance to help cover transport costs at the beginning and end of the fellowship What is Not Covered? Applicants should budget separately for: Medical and travel insurance Visa and passport costs Laboratory or bench fees Daily commuting costs (covered within the subsistence allowance) Travel or expenses for accompanying family members Taxes and bank charges where applicable To apply for a research fellowship award 1. please consult the following documents: Guidelines and Conditions for CRP Research Fellowship Awards Selection Criteria for CRP Fellowship Awards Action Areas of the Co-operative Research Programme Sample fellowship applications: Example 1 ; Example 2 Sample CV Sample invitation letter PDF Fellowship Application form 2. complete the following document: Online application form for CRP Research Fellowships National correspondents and country files The Co-operative Research Programme (CRP) currently has 32 participating countries with Brazil joining on 1 July 2026 . Below is a list of countries, the contact information for their national correspondents and when available a [Country file], document which gives general information on the country s research institutes and universities. Australia Dr. Andy SHEPPARD Austria Ms. Sanda PASC-WANDL [ Country file ] Belgium Mme Véronique DEWASMES [ Country file ] Brazil Dr. Maria José A. M. SAMPAIO [ Country file ] Canada Ms Melanie SINGARAYER Chile Mr Jack BRADY [ Country file ] Colombia Mr. Felipe CORREAL CHARRIS Costa Rica Mr. José Fabián JIMÉNEZ MORALES Czechia Ms. Šárka ŠTEJNAROVÁ [ Country file ] Denmark Mr. Bjarne THOMSEN Estonia Dr. Katri LING [ Country file ] Finland Prof. Pirjo PELTONEN-SAINIO [ Country file ] France Dr. Joachim HUET Germany Prof. Dr. Jens DAUBER Hungary Prof. András SZÉKÁCS Ireland Ms. Ellen DURKIN Israel Dr. Michal LEVY [Country file ] Italy Ms. Silvia BARALLA Japan Dr. Ichiro NAKAYAMA Korea Ms Da-Som KANG Latvia Ms Diāna LĪVA [ Country file] Lithuania Ms Vilma KRAUJALYTE [ Country file ] Netherlands Ms. Saske HOVING [ Country file ] New Zealand Ms. Shania BROOKS [ Country file ] Norway Dr. Maan Singh SIDHU [ Country file] Slovak Republic Dr. Kristína GENDOVÁ RUZSÍKOVÁ [ Country file ] Spain Dr. Violeta CARRASCO Sweden Dr. Sara GRÄSLUND [ Country file ] Switzerland Ms Veronica CAGGIA Türkiye Dr. Fatih ÖZDEMİR United Kingdom Mr. Alen BIJU [ Country file ] United States Ms. Marcella WITTING How Applications Are Evaluated Applications are assessed against several selection criteria. Strong proposals should clearly demonstrate: Scientific excellence and innovation Relevance to the OECD CRP Action Areas A feasible and well-designed research plan Long-term collaboration between the applicant and host institution Strong scientific achievements and publication record Multidisciplinary approaches Plans for dissemination of research findings Practical impact of the research Clear policy relevance for agriculture, forestry or fisheries New international collaborations are given preference over existing collaborations Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Completed online application form Curriculum Vitae (CV) List of publications from the past five years Signed invitation letter from the host institution Research proposal addressing all selection criteria Applications must be submitted in English . Fellowship Timeline Applications close: 10 September 2026 Award decisions: Mid-December 2026 Successful applicants notified: By January 2027 Recommended fellowship start: From 1 March 2027 Latest fellowship departure: 15 December 2027 Responsibilities of Successful Fellows Recipients of the fellowship will be expected to: Confirm arrival at the host institution Complete an evaluation questionnaire after the fellowship Submit a summary report within two months of completing the research Acknowledge OECD CRP funding in any publications arising from the fellowship Failure to submit the required final report may result in repayment of 10% of the fellowship award . Contact For any question regarding a fellowship application, please contact the CRP Secretariat: TAD.Prog@oecd.org Source: https://www.oecd.org/en/about/programmes/co-operative-research-programme/crp-applications-for-fellowships.html The post OECD Research Fellowships Research Fellowships 2027 | Fully Funded International Research Fellowships for Agricultural, Forestry Fisheries Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ASIF Ignite Africa Cohort 1 2026: 8-Week Remote Accelerator for African Climate-Tech Startups Building Food & Agriculture Solutions
summary: Applications are now open for the ASIF Ignite Africa Cohort 1 , an intensive 8-week fully remote venture support programme designed specifically for early-stage founders developing climate adaptation and resilience solutions across Africa s food and agriculture value chains. The programme supports founders who have already built a working prototype and are ready to validate their ideas with real users. Rather than focusing on theoretical business planning, Ignite helps startups move from assumptions to evidence by testing products in real-world environments, gathering customer feedback, refining business models, and preparing ventures for future investment and growth. Unlike many startup accelerators that focus primarily on scaling businesses, ASIF Ignite places climate resilience at the center of innovation. The programme supports solutions that help vulnerable communities, especially smallholder farmers, better prepare for, withstand, recover from, and adapt to the growing impacts of climate change. Applications remain open until 17 August 2026 . About ASIF Ignite Africa ASIF Ignite is a cohort-based venture development programme that brings together promising early-stage founders from across Africa to validate climate adaptation innovations. The programme is designed for entrepreneurs who have already moved beyond the idea stage by building a tangible product, service, technology, application, platform, device, or operating model, but who have not yet tested it extensively with their intended users. Throughout the eight-week programme, participants receive structured guidance to: Validate customer demand Conduct real-world testing Learn Lean Startup methodologies Explore practical AI applications Build measurable evidence of impact Improve investor readiness Strengthen business models The programme is delivered entirely online, making it accessible to founders across Africa. Why This Programme Matters Climate change continues to place enormous pressure on Africa s food systems. Smallholder farmers increasingly face: Droughts Unpredictable rainfall Flooding Soil degradation Crop losses Poor market access Rising temperatures Food spoilage Supply chain disruptions Across Sub-Saharan Africa, up to one-third of food produced is lost before reaching consumers due to inadequate storage, poor logistics, and climate-related disruptions. ASIF Ignite seeks founders developing practical innovations that improve resilience throughout the agricultural value chain. Programme Focus for Africa Cohort 1 The first African cohort specifically focuses on food and agriculture value chains . This includes every stage involved in producing food, including: Farm inputs Production Water management Farm technologies Storage Transportation Processing Distribution Market access The programme supports solutions that enable farmers and agribusinesses to prepare for climate shocks while maintaining sustainable livelihoods. Solution Areas Supported The programme welcomes innovations across multiple areas. 1. Inputs and Agricultural Knowledge Solutions that improve farmers access to critical resources and information, including: Climate-resilient seeds Improved planting materials Farm machinery Irrigation systems Greenhouse technologies Agricultural input financing Input logistics Supply chain innovations Weather-index insurance Microcredit Climate forecasting Early warning systems Agronomic advisory services Solar-powered farm energy Energy-efficient farming technologies 2. Growing Crops More Sustainably Innovations that help farmers produce more while using fewer resources. Examples include: Hydroponics Aquaponics Solar irrigation Farm management software Controlled-environment agriculture Remote sensing Agricultural drones Smart sensors Farm automation technologies 3. Protecting Land and Water Solutions that strengthen natural ecosystems and long-term agricultural productivity. Examples include: Sustainable fisheries Fishery management Agroforestry Land restoration Regenerative agriculture Soil health improvement 4. Post-Harvest Management Technologies that reduce food loss after harvesting. Examples include: Cold storage Refrigeration Warehousing Aggregation centres Agricultural hubs Market access platforms Trading platforms 5. Processing and Market Access The programme also welcomes processing and sales innovations when they directly improve climate resilience for smallholder farmers. Examples include: Solar-powered milling Low-energy food processing Distribution systems Market access technologies Processing solutions that only improve operational efficiency without benefiting smallholder farmers are not eligible. AI is Encouraged but Not Required One unique aspect of ASIF Ignite is its practical approach to Artificial Intelligence. Applicants do not need prior AI knowledge. Instead, founders are encouraged to learn how AI can strengthen existing products. Possible AI applications include: Satellite data analysis Crop disease detection Credit scoring for farmers Weather forecasting Personalized farming advice Automated insurance claims Local-language agricultural advisory services Whether AI becomes the main product or simply enhances an existing solution, participants receive practical guidance on integrating it effectively. Who Should Apply? The programme is open to: Early-stage startups MSMEs Founder-led ventures Social enterprises Climate technology entrepreneurs Applicants should: Have developed a functional prototype Be preparing for real-world testing Focus on climate adaptation Operate within food and agriculture value chains Have or be building a sustainable revenue model Businesses primarily dependent on grants or donations are not eligible. Company registration is optional during application but should be completed before programme completion whenever possible. What Stage Should Your Startup Be? Ideal applicants should have already built a prototype. Examples include: A solar-powered cold room ready for market testing. A weather advisory application ready for use by farmers. A satellite-based credit scoring platform prepared for pilot deployment. Solutions should have progressed beyond laboratory demonstrations and be ready for testing with actual users. Who Is Not Eligible? The following are generally not suitable: Businesses without climate adaptation relevance. Projects focused solely on reducing emissions without helping communities adapt. Products designed only to increase yields without improving resilience. Traditional processing businesses with no benefit to smallholder farmers. Large corporations with mature commercial products. Businesses operating outside food and agriculture. Processing or sales businesses that improve efficiency alone without strengthening farmer resilience. What Participants Receive Selected founders will gain access to an extensive support package including: Weekly live workshops Customer discovery training Lean experimentation methods AI implementation guidance Impact measurement support One-on-one mentoring Office hours with AI specialists Product experts Agricultural sector advisors Temporary access to premium digital tools Playbooks for testing solutions in climate-vulnerable communities Feedback from investors and industry experts Outcomes After Graduation By the end of the programme, founders will leave with: A stronger business model AI-enabled product improvements Real customer validation Evidence of market demand Measurable impact data Improved investor readiness A refined pitch for future funding opportunities Outstanding ventures may be identified for future Propel cohorts, although graduation from Ignite does not guarantee automatic progression. Programme Format The accelerator consists of: 8-week programme Fully remote delivery Approximately 13–14 founders per cohort Two live workshop sessions every week Additional asynchronous assignments between sessions To support founders balancing work or caregiving responsibilities, workshop content can be recorded for later viewing. Programme Timeline Months 1–3 Applications Review process Cohort selection Months 4–5 Onboarding Workshops AI learning Mentorship Product development Months 5–6 Validation Evidence building Showcase Graduation Expert feedback Important Dates Application Deadline: 17 August 2026 Why You Should Apply If you are building a climate innovation capable of helping African farmers adapt to climate change, this programme provides a rare opportunity to validate your solution with expert guidance before scaling. Instead of simply teaching entrepreneurship, ASIF Ignite helps founders generate the evidence investors, customers, and partners want to see while strengthening products through customer feedback and practical AI integration. Founders who complete the programme will be better positioned to attract funding, scale responsibly, and deliver measurable climate resilience across African food systems. How to Apply Interested applicants can learn more and apply for the ASIF Ignite Africa Cohort 1 2026 HERE . Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . The post ASIF Ignite Africa Cohort 1 2026: 8-Week Remote Accelerator for African Climate-Tech Startups Building Food Agriculture Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: PILnet-RLRH Fellowship 2026: Fully Remote International Legal Fellowship Supporting Refugee Leaders | Up to 12 Months of Professional Development and Public Interest Law Experience
summary: The PILnet-RLRH Fellowship 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for emerging legal professionals and refugee leaders to gain hands-on experience in public interest law while contributing to one of the world s most impactful initiatives supporting refugee-led organizations. Organized through a partnership between PILnet and the Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH) at the Refugee Studies Centre, University of Oxford , this international remote fellowship is designed to empower individuals whose lived experiences and legal expertise can help transform access to justice for displaced communities around the world. The fellowship offers participants the opportunity to conduct meaningful legal research, influence refugee policy discussions, strengthen professional leadership skills, and collaborate with internationally recognized experts working on forced displacement and refugee rights. Applications are currently open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply before the 15 July 2026 deadline. About the PILnet-RLRH Fellowship The PILnet-RLRH Fellowship was established to address an important gap within the legal and civil society sectors. Many talented refugee leaders and professionals with lived experience of displacement possess valuable knowledge but often face barriers to accessing leadership opportunities, professional networks, mentorship, and career advancement. This fellowship recognizes that lived experience is a powerful form of expertise. Rather than viewing displaced persons solely as beneficiaries, the fellowship positions them as researchers, legal professionals, advocates, and leaders capable of shaping international conversations surrounding refugee protection and access to justice. Successful fellows will follow the path of previous fellows such as Lidya Tewelde Habtay and Salah Jawish, contributing to PILnet s growing work supporting refugee-led organizations globally. Fellowship Overview Organization PILnet Partner Organization Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH), Refugee Studies Centre, University of Oxford Position PILnet-RLRH Legal Fellow Location Fully Remote Applicants must remain based in: Africa Europe The Americas Duration Up to 12 months Work Schedule 20–24 hours per week Approximately 3–4 working days weekly Working schedules will be agreed upon at the beginning of the fellowship. Start Date Flexible Expected from August 2026 Earlier or later start dates may be arranged depending on the fellow s availability. Financial Support Part-time stipend of £540 (subject to adjustment depending on location). Application Deadline Deadline: 15 July 2026 Applicants will receive notification of the outcome approximately 3–4 weeks after the application deadline. Fellowship Objectives The fellowship seeks to develop future leaders within refugee advocacy by focusing on several important objectives: Center lived experience alongside professional legal expertise. Strengthen leadership and strategic thinking. Expand professional mentoring opportunities. Build lasting international professional networks. Increase participation of refugee leaders in legal research and advocacy. Support PILnet s forced displacement initiatives. Improve access to justice for refugee-led organizations. Fellowship Responsibilities Participants will work across four major areas throughout the fellowship. 1. Mentorship and Professional Development Each fellow will: Develop an Individual Development Plan. Work closely with mentors. Receive ongoing guidance throughout the fellowship. Build long-term leadership skills. 2. Lead an International Legal Research Project The fellow will lead a significant research project expanding previous work conducted by former PILnet-RLRH fellows. The research focuses on understanding the legal needs of Refugee-Led Organizations (RLOs). Previous studies gathered evidence from: Uganda Egypt South Africa Australia alongside NGO representatives, academic experts, and PILnet s Refugee-Led Clearinghouse data collected between 2020 and 2024. The 2026 fellow will expand this work by including perspectives from the Americas , filling an important geographical gap identified in earlier research. Main Research Objectives The project includes two major goals: 1. Update Existing Research The fellow will: Review previous findings. Incorporate new legal developments. Analyze updated Refugee-Led Clearinghouse data. Integrate lessons learned since the original publication. 2. Expand Research to the Americas The fellow will: Conduct primary research with Refugee-LLed Organizations. Interview refugee leaders. Engage NGOs. Consult legal practitioners. Produce updated comparative global analysis. Research Activities The fellow is expected to: Conduct semi-structured interviews. Collect qualitative research. Analyze legal needs affecting refugee-led organizations. Produce an updated international report. Prepare additional legal tools or policy briefs where appropriate. Coordinate closely with PILnet staff and the Refugee-Led Research Hub. Professional Development Opportunities Beyond research, fellows will benefit from: Leadership development. Professional mentoring. International networking. Workshops. Events. Specialized training. Collaboration with global legal professionals. Exposure to public interest law practice. Organizational Contributions Throughout the fellowship, participants will also contribute to: PILnet s Refugee Clearinghouse. Discussions surrounding forced displacement programming. Strategic initiatives related to refugee access to justice. Cross-organizational collaboration. Fellowship Deliverables Participants will complete several outputs during the fellowship, including: Individual Development Plan Agreed research deliverable Mid-program reflection Final presentation Written reflection report Expected Learning Outcomes By the end of the fellowship, participants will have: Stronger leadership capacity. Improved strategic and programmatic thinking. Greater understanding of forced displacement issues. Advanced knowledge of refugee legal needs. Practical experience in public interest law. Enhanced skills in pro bono partnership building. Expanded global professional networks. Experience producing high-quality legal research. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following criteria: Essential Requirements Lived experience of forced displacement or current/former Mastercard Foundation Scholar. Undergraduate or graduate legal degree, or significant legal experience. Active leadership in Refugee-Led Organizations or refugee advocacy. Demonstrated impact on refugee-related policies, programs, or practices. Excellent written and spoken English. Strong collaboration skills. Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of the global refugee protection system. Additional language proficiency. About PILnet PILnet is an international non-governmental organization that partners with legal professionals and civil society organizations worldwide to strengthen justice systems and civic participation. Its work focuses on three strategic priorities: Civic Space Climate Justice Forced Displacement Through partnerships, legal expertise, and professional development, PILnet works toward building more equitable, inclusive, and sustainable societies. About the Refugee-Led Research Hub (RLRH) The Refugee-Led Research Hub supports individuals with lived experience of displacement to become leaders across multiple disciplines, including: Law Human Rights Politics Forced Migration Research Public Policy Since its establishment, the organization has built an international community of more than 1,400 researchers, students, and professionals across over 100 countries. Its active offices include: Nairobi Oxford Kakuma Refugee Camp Kampala Kuala Lumpur Oxfordshire One of the Hub s defining features is that the majority of its leadership team has lived experience of displacement, ensuring refugee voices remain central to its mission. Information Session Interested applicants can learn more during the online information session. Date: 6 July 2026 Time: 2:30 PM (UK Time) The session introduces applicants to: PILnet Fellowship responsibilities Application process Questions and answers Registration Link: https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/SBTTM4oOTQqX1Jj6yITpHA How to Apply Eligible candidates should prepare their application before the closing date of 15 July 2026 . Interested applicants can learn more and apply for the PILnet-RLRH Fellowship 2026 HERE . Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . The post PILnet-RLRH Fellowship 2026: Fully Remote International Legal Fellowship Supporting Refugee Leaders | Up to 12 Months of Professional Development and Public Interest Law Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
